911_964.name: Porsche 911 (964)
notable_features: Coil spring suspension replaced torsion bars
fun_facts: The 964 was the first major 911 redesign since the original
Carrera 4 launched before the Carrera 2
The 964 Turbo S is among the most sought-after air-cooled 911s

911_993.name: Porsche 911 (993)
fun_facts: The 993 is often considered the most beautiful 911 ever made
993 values have skyrocketed as the last air-cooled generation
The 993 GT2 is one of the most valuable modern Porsches

996.name: Porsche 911 (996)
fun_facts: The 996 was controversial for abandoning air-cooling
IMS bearing failures affected a small percentage but became legendary
The 996 GT3 RS is now a highly collectible car

997_1.name: Porsche 911 (997.1)
notable_features: PASM adaptive suspension standard on S
First 911 GT3 RS sold in US
fun_facts: The 997 was the best-selling 911 at launch
997.1 still used the IMS bearing but improved design

997_2.name: Porsche 911 (997.2)
fun_facts: The switch to DFI eliminated the dreaded IMS bearing
The GT3 RS 4.0 is one of the most valuable modern 911s
The 997 Sport Classic was limited to 250 units

991_1.name: Porsche 911 (991.1)
fun_facts: The 991 was significantly larger than any previous 911
Introduced the 7-speed manual with short top gear for fuel economy
991 GT3 was PDK only, causing controversy

991_2.name: Porsche 911 (991.2)
notable_features: Twin-turbo engines for base Carrera models
fun_facts: Purists lamented the turbo Carrera but performance improved dramatically
The 991.2 GT3 came with manual trans option after fan outcry
The 991.2 Speedster was the last NA engine in a non-GT 911

992.name: Porsche 911 (992)
notable_features: Wet mode detects water on road
fun_facts: The 992 has 45% more computing power than previous gen
Every 992 Carrera is now as wide as old Carrera 4S was
The 992 GT3 set a 6:55.2 Nurburgring lap time

986_boxster.name: Porsche Boxster (986)
fun_facts: The Boxster is credited with saving Porsche from bankruptcy
Name comes from Boxer engine + Roadster
Shared IMS bearing concern with 996

987_boxster.name: Porsche Boxster (987)
notable_features: 987.2 got DFI engines eliminating IMS concern
fun_facts: The 987 Boxster Spyder was the lightest Porsche you could buy
987.2 DFI engines are considered very reliable

987_cayman.name: Porsche Cayman (987)
notable_features: Fixed roof coupe version of Boxster
Cayman R was lightweight track special
fun_facts: Porsche allegedly held back the Cayman to not embarrass the 911
Named after the Caiman crocodilian
The Cayman R is considered one of the best driver's cars ever made

981_boxster.name: Porsche Boxster (981)
notable_features: Last NA flat-six Boxster (before 718 went turbo 4)
fun_facts: The 981 is considered one of the best-handling Boxsters ever
Boxster Spyder used the GT3 engine

981_cayman.name: Porsche Cayman (981)
notable_features: Last NA flat-six Cayman before 718 turbo 4
fun_facts: The Cayman GT4 was so good it upset the 911 pecking order
Many consider the 981 Cayman the best sports car for the money

718_boxster.name: Porsche 718 Boxster
notable_features: GTS 4.0 and Spyder returned to flat-6
Final year models got flat-6 across range
fun_facts: The 718 name honors the 1957-1962 Porsche 718 race car
Purists hated the turbo-4 sound but loved the performance
The 718 Spyder with 4.0L flat-6 is considered a modern classic

718_cayman.name: Porsche 718 Cayman
notable_features: Turbo flat-4 replaced NA flat-6 for base/S
GT4 retained flat-6 naturally aspirated engine
fun_facts: The 718 Cayman GT4 RS is faster than many 911 variants
Flat-4 turbo was controversial but improved mid-range torque
GT4 RS uses the 911 GT3's 4.0L flat-6 with individual throttle bodies

cayenne_955.name: Porsche Cayenne (955)
fun_facts: Purists were outraged when Porsche announced an SUV
The Cayenne quickly became Porsche's best-selling model
Named after cayenne pepper

cayenne_957.name: Porsche Cayenne (957)
fun_facts: The Cayenne GTS combined sport and SUV like no other
DFI direct injection improved both power and economy

cayenne_958.name: Porsche Cayenne (958)
notable_features: Up to 570 hp in Turbo S
fun_facts: The 958 was significantly lighter than the 957
Cayenne E-Hybrid was Porsche's first plug-in hybrid production car

cayenne_e3.name: Porsche Cayenne (E3)
notable_features: MLB Evo platform shared with Audi Q7/Q8
Up to 670 hp in Turbo GT
fun_facts: The Cayenne Turbo GT set a 7:38.9 Nurburgring SUV record
Cayenne Coupe competes with BMW X6 and Mercedes GLE Coupe
